name: Wrist Roller
resistance_profile: A weighted rope wound around a bar trains the wrist flexors and extensors through repeated flexion and extension under continuous tension until the weight reaches the top.
cues: Keep the elbows fixed out in front of you
Control the descent, do not let it free-fall
execution_steps: Hold the roller bar with both hands, palms down, arms extended in front at about shoulder height.
Wind the rope upward by alternately flexing and extending the wrists to lift the weight to the bar.
Once the weight reaches the top, reverse the wrist action and unwind it back down under control.
Keep the elbows relatively still so the wrists do all the work.
good_when: You want to train wrist flexors and extensors together in a single continuous set
You want a high time-under-tension grip and forearm finisher
bad_when: Shoulder or front-delt fatigue forces you to stop before the forearms are worked
You need finely tuned progressive overload, since load jumps are hard to control
common_errors: Swinging the arms or using the shoulders to lift the weight
Letting the weight drop instead of unwinding it under control
progressions: Slow the eccentric unwind for more time under tension
regressions: Rest the elbows on a rack or bench to remove shoulder fatigue
